content.full_text:
California State Senate

FOURTH DISTRICT :

BUTTE COUNTY
COLUSA COUNTY
GLENN COUNTY
LAKE COUNTY
NAPA COUNTY
SHASTA COUNTY
SONOMA COUNTY (PART)
TEHAMA COUNTY

COMMITTEES:
AGRICULTURE AND WATER
RESOURCES
APPROPRIATIONS
INSURANCE, CLAIMS AND
WORKER'S COMP., CHAIRMAN
NATURAL RESOURCES AND
WILDLIFE
TOXICS AND PUBLIC
SAFETY MANAGEMENT

SELECT COMMITTEES:
UPPER SACRAMENTO VALLEY
ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
RANGELAND ISSUES, CHAIRMAN
AIDS
CALIFORNIA'S WINE INDUSTRY
PACIFIC RIM
SENATE CAUCUS
VICE CHAIRMAN
STATE ALLOCATION BOARD

SENATOR JIM NIELSEN

1 June 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814-814

Attention: Technical Assistance and
Analysis

To Whom It May Concern:

Per my phone conversation with Mary Ann today, regarding
a recent contribution we received, I requested her
response in writing for our records. Mary Ann informed me
that I must make a formal written request to you.

My question to Mary Ann is as follows: We received a
contribution from a company where the husband and wife are
equal partners. Our understanding of a situation like
this is that because they have their own business and are
equal partners they can only contribute a total of $1,000
in a fiscal year. They loose their ability to each
contribute $1,000 per fiscal year. Since talking with
Mary Ann I find this is incorrect. Would you please
clarify this misunderstanding for us.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

Gayle Risberg
Friends of Jim Nielsen

gr

Friends of Jim Nielsen, P.O. Box 1202, Sacramento, CA 95812-1202 • (916) 443-6046 • ID # 890121
Not printed or mailed at government expense.

embedding.qa_text: The letter is from Senator Jim Nielsen, dated 1 June 1990, to the Fair Political Practices Commission. It addresses a misunderstanding regarding contribution limits for equal partners in a business. The senator explains that he received a contribution from a company where the husband and wife are equal partners and wants clarification on whether they can contribute a total of $1,000 per fiscal year or if each partner can contribute $1,000 individually. The senator asks for technical assistance and analysis. The letter concludes with a signature from Gayle Risberg, who is associated with Friends of Jim Nielsen.
